content.text: For Kit at 8, the **Canadian Paediatric Society** shifts the framing away from a hard daily minute cap and toward *consistency and quality* — so rather than a fixed number, what tends to work well is agreeing on predictable limits ahead of the weekend (e.g., a set window in the afternoon after outdoor time or reading), watching or playing together when you can, and keeping screens off at least an hour before bed. A practical starting point many families use is 1–2 hours of recreational screen time per day on weekends, but the CPS guidance is clear that how it's used — whether it's passive scrolling vs. a game you play together, and whether it crowds out sleep, movement, or face-to-face time — matters more than the number itself. If you'd like, share what your current weekend rhythm looks like and I can help you build a structure that fits Kit's routine specifically.
